components.name: GridState
components.responsibility: Represents the game grid; owns snake body segments, food position, and provides query methods
public_interface: snake: [number, number][] (getter - array of [x, y] coordinates)
state_owned: snake: array of [x, y] head and body segments
components.justification: FR-1, FR-3: Grid state must persist between ticks and be queried by collision detector and renderer

components.name: SnakeEngine
components.responsibility: Handles snake movement logic; updates head position based on direction, grows on food consumption, maintains body segments
public_interface: move(direction: Direction, foodPos: [number, number]): { headX: number, headY: number, grew: boolean }
components.justification: FR-1: Core snake movement mechanics; FR-5: Must support speed increases as snake grows

components.name: CollisionDetector
components.responsibility: Detects collisions between snake head and walls or body; determines game-over conditions
public_interface: checkWallCollision(x: number, y: number, gridWidth: number, gridHeight: number): boolean
components.justification: FR-2: Collision detection for walls and self-body; determines when game ends

components.name: FoodSpawner
components.responsibility: Spawns food at random empty grid cells; ensures food does not spawn on snake body
public_interface: spawn(snakeBody: [number, number][], gridWidth: number, gridHeight: number): [number, number]
components.justification: FR-3: Food must appear at random empty cells, never on snake

components.name: GameState
components.responsibility: Tracks game-level state: score, speed, game status (playing/paused/ended), current generation
components.justification: FR-4: Score tracking; FR-5: Speed management based on snake length

components.name: InputHandler
components.responsibility: Buffers arrow key input to prevent reverse direction in single move; provides direction queue for engine
components.justification: FR-11: Prevent rapid key presses from causing reverse direction; only process latest buffered direction per tick

components.name: StateSerializer
components.responsibility: Captures and serializes complete game state for replay recording; enables bit-exact playback
public_interface: serialize(gameState: GameState, gridState: GridState, generation: number): SerializedState
deserialize(data: SerializedState): { gameState: GameState, gridState: GridState }
components.justification: FR-6: Must record all game states for accurate replay; serialization enables persistence to localStorage

components.name: ReplayRecorder
components.responsibility: Records complete game from start to end; stores in memory; provides access to replay list
components.justification: FR-6, FR-7: Must record all states and persist completed games for replay selection

components.name: ReplayEngine
components.responsibility: Plays back recorded game states at variable speeds (0.5×, 1×, 2×, 4×); generates ghost snake for overlay
components.justification: FR-7: Playback at variable speeds; FR-9: Generates independent state for ghost snake overlay

components.name: CanvasRenderer
components.responsibility: Renders live game and/or ghost replay on HTML5 Canvas; manages colors, cell size, full/partial redraws
public_interface: drawSnake(snake: [number, number][], color: string, opacity?: number): void
setGridDimensions(width: number, height: number, cellSize: number): void
pixelToGridCell(px: number, py: number): [number, number] | null
components.justification: NFR-2: 60 FPS rendering on canvas; FR-8: Ghost snake rendered with visual distinction; FR-9: Simultaneous live and ghost rendering

components.name: TickScheduler
components.responsibility: Manages RAF loop; gates tick execution based on speed setting; coordinates game state updates
components.justification: Core game loop; coordinates all engine updates per tick

components.name: UIController
components.responsibility: Wires all DOM events to game engine; manages button states; coordinates display updates; handles game flow (new/pause/resume/stop/replay)
public_interface: onTick(generation: number, gameState: GameState, gridState: GridState): void
components.justification: Central wiring layer between user interactions and game engine; coordinates all subsystems

data_flow: User presses arrow key → InputHandler buffers direction
TickScheduler.tick() → SnakeEngine.move(direction) → GridState updated with new snake position
CollisionDetector.checkCollisions() → determines game-over or food consumption
StateSerializer.serialize() → current state captured for replay
ReplayRecorder.recordTick() → state added to recording array
CanvasRenderer.draw() → renders live snake, food, ghost snake from ReplayEngine
On game end → ReplayRecorder finishes recording, saves to localStorage
On replay selection → ReplayEngine loads states, generates ghost snake in parallel with live game

types.SerializedState: { snake: [number, number][], food: [number, number], score: number, generation: number, timestamp: number }
types.ReplayData: { id: string, timestamp: number, score: number, states: SerializedState[], duration: number }
types.ReplayMetadata: { id: string, timestamp: number, score: number, duration: number }

architectural_principles: Separation of concerns: engine (logic), replay (state recording/playback), render (display), ui (wiring)
Immutability for replay states: each tick produces new SerializedState, old states preserved for replay
Deterministic replay: same input sequence produces identical sequence of game states (no randomness in moves)
Independent systems: live game ticking and replay playback operate independently without interference
Single source of truth: GridState and GameState are canonical; replica state only in serialization

unit_tests: SnakeEngine: movement in 4 directions, growth on food, boundary wrapping
CollisionDetector: wall collision, self-collision, food collision detection
FoodSpawner: random spawn on empty cells, never on snake
GameState: score increment, speed calculation based on length
InputHandler: buffering, direction changes, no reverse direction in rapid presses
ReplayEngine: frame advancement, speed multiplier calculation
integration_tests: Complete game flow: start → move → eat → grow → speed increase → collision → end
Replay recording: all ticks recorded, replay states match original
Input buffering: rapid key presses prevent invalid moves
Live + ghost: simultaneous play without interference
e2e_tests: Launch game → move snake with arrow keys → eat food → verify score and speed
Complete game → verify saved to replay list
Start new game → start ghost replay → verify coexistence and no collision logic
Verify bit-exactness: replay produces same sequence of states
